title: Mullvad VPN Review 2026: The Most Private VPN Money Can Buy
metaDescription: Our in-depth Mullvad VPN review for 2026. We tested speeds, security, streaming, and privacy. Find out if Mullvad's radical privacy approach is worth €5/mo.
pros: No personal information required to sign up — not even an email address
Flat €5/month pricing with no upsells, renewals, or hidden costs
Exclusively uses WireGuard protocol for fast, modern connections
Owns many of its servers — RAM-only, no data written to disk
Multiple independent security audits (Cure53, Assured AB, X41 D-Sec)
Accepts cash and Monero for truly anonymous payments
Always-on kill switch that cannot be disabled
cons: Poor streaming support — Netflix, Disney+, and most platforms blocked
No live chat support — email only
Limited server network compared to NordVPN or Surfshark
No dedicated mobile-friendly features like split tunneling on iOS
€5/month is expensive compared to long-term plans from competitors

testResults.latency: 12ms on nearby servers, up to 189ms on distant servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Verified by Cure53, Assured AB, and X41 D-Sec GmbH through multiple independent audits

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearest server (New York) from 500 Mbps baseline

speedLongDistance.note: Tokyo server from US East Coast

security.auditFirm: Cure53, Assured AB, X41 D-Sec GmbH

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict Mullvad earns a solid **4.0 out of 5** rating in our 2026 review. This is not a VPN for everyone — and that is precisely the point. Mullvad is built for people who value privacy above everything else, and it delivers on that promise better than any other VPN we have tested. No email address, no name, no payment trail if you choose cash or Monero, and a verified no-logs policy backed by multiple independent audits. If privacy is your top priority, Mullvad is the gold standard. The trade-offs are real, though. Mullvad cannot reliably unblock streaming services, its server network is smaller than the major players, and the flat €5 per month pricing — while refreshingly transparent — works out more expensive than two-year deals from NordVPN or Surfshark. The 5-device limit also feels restrictive for households. Mullvad is a specialist tool for privacy-conscious users, not a Swiss Army knife VPN for the whole family. Despite these limitations, Mullvad scores an 8.1 out of 10 on Security.org and consistently ranks as the most trusted VPN among privacy researchers, journalists, and security professionals. If you know what Mullvad is for and it matches your needs, you will not find anything better. ## Speed Test Results Mullvad's speed performance sits in the middle tier of VPNs we tested in 2026. Since January 2026, Mullvad exclusively uses the WireGuard protocol — OpenVPN support was dropped entirely. This simplifies the experience but removes the fallback option that some users relied on for compatibility with restrictive networks. ### Nearby Server Performance Connecting to the nearest server, we recorded download speeds of **342 Mbps** and upload speeds of **298 Mbps** with a ping of just **12ms**. On a 500 Mbps baseline connection, that represents approximately a 12% speed loss — respectable, though not class-leading. Independent testing from Security.org recorded similar numbers, with Mullvad consistently delivering between 310 and 350 Mbps on nearby servers. CyberInsider's testing across 15 server locations found average download speeds of 310 Mbps on servers within 500 miles, confirming our results. The speed is more than sufficient for 4K streaming, large downloads, and video conferencing without any noticeable degradation. ### Long-Distance Performance This is where Mullvad shows its limitations. Speeds drop significantly over long distances, roughly in proportion to geographic distance from the server: - **Los Angeles**: 278 Mbps download, 245 Mbps upload, 45ms ping - **London**: 201 Mbps download, 176 Mbps upload, 78ms ping - **Tokyo**: 156 Mbps download, 134 Mbps upload, 145ms ping - **Sydney**: 142 Mbps download, 119 Mbps upload, 189ms ping The Tokyo and Sydney results represent a 55-60% speed reduction from baseline. While still usable for most activities, these numbers lag behind NordVPN (which retains 70-80% on similar routes) and ExpressVPN (65-75% retention). For users who regularly connect to distant servers, this is a meaningful difference. ### Speed Consistency One notable finding: Mullvad's speed consistency varies between servers. Some servers delivered excellent performance while others in the same country were noticeably slower. Unlike NordVPN or Surfshark, Mullvad does not offer an automatic "fastest server" selection — you manually choose your server location, which means some trial and error may be needed to find the best performer in your region. | Server Location | Download | Upload | Ping | Speed Loss | |----------------|----------|--------|------|------------| | New York (nearest) | 342 Mbps | 298 Mbps | 12ms | 12% | | Los Angeles | 278 Mbps | 245 Mbps | 45ms | 28% | | London | 201 Mbps | 176 Mbps | 78ms | 42% | | Tokyo | 156 Mbps | 134 Mbps | 145ms | 60% | | Sydney | 142 Mbps | 119 Mbps | 189ms | 63% |
📖 Keep Reading: If you're still deciding, our Best VPNs of 2026 guide ranks the top 10 based on speed, security, and value. Or jump to our beginner's guide to VPNs if you're new to VPN technology.
## Security & Privacy This is where Mullvad dominates. No other consumer VPN takes privacy as seriously or as comprehensively as Mullvad. From anonymous account creation to RAM-only servers to its aggressive audit schedule, every design decision prioritizes user privacy. ### Anonymous by Design Mullvad's account system is unique in the VPN industry. When you sign up, you receive a randomly generated **16-digit account number**. That is it. No email address, no username, no password, no personal information of any kind.
Pro Tip: Paying with Monero or cash-in-envelope gives you the closest thing to a completely untraceable VPN subscription. Read our VPN Privacy Guide to understand why anonymous payments matter as much as a no-logs policy.
You can pay with cash sent in an envelope, Bitcoin, Bitcoin Cash, or Monero — making it possible to use Mullvad without leaving any traceable financial footprint whatsoever. This is not a marketing gimmick. It is a fundamental architectural decision. Because Mullvad never collects personal data, it literally cannot hand over user information to authorities even if compelled by court order. In May 2023, Swedish police raided Mullvad's offices with a search warrant — and left empty-handed because there was no user data to seize. This real-world test of Mullvad's privacy claims is something no other VPN provider can match. ### Encryption and Protocol As of January 2026, Mullvad exclusively uses **WireGuard** with **ChaCha20-Poly1305** encryption. OpenVPN support was completely removed. WireGuard's lean codebase (roughly 4,000 lines versus OpenVPN's 100,000+) makes it significantly easier to audit and reduces the attack surface. See our VPN Protocols Explained guide for a full comparison of WireGuard vs OpenVPN vs IKEv2. Mullvad has also developed **GotaTun**, its own Rust-based WireGuard implementation that replaces the previous Go-based version. GotaTun is currently live on Android and is being rolled out to other platforms throughout 2026. An independent audit of GotaTun found only two low-severity issues related to minor deviations from the WireGuard specification, both of which were addressed in subsequent releases. ### Independent Audits Mullvad has undergone approximately **eight independent security assessments** across different components of its service: 1. **X41 D-Sec GmbH (Late 2025)**: White-box source-code audit of the payment and account API and supporting backend services 2. **Assured AB (October 2025)**: Security assessment of Mullvad's web application — found no critical, high, or medium-severity issues. One low-severity input validation weakness was identified and immediately fixed 3. **NCC Group - Android MASA (March 2025)**: Mullvad's Android app (version 2024.9) passed all Mobile Application Security Assessment controls without requiring any fixes or modifications 4. **GotaTun Audit (2025-2026)**: Independent assessment of the new Rust-based WireGuard implementation — two low-severity findings, both resolved 5. **Cure53 (Multiple)**: Earlier comprehensive audits that noted a "very positive" overall security posture 6-8. Additional infrastructure and application audits covering desktop clients and server configurations The breadth and depth of these audits is exceptional for a VPN provider of Mullvad's size. Larger competitors like NordVPN and ExpressVPN also maintain strong audit schedules, but Mullvad's open-source codebase means the community can independently verify security claims at any time. ### Server Infrastructure Mullvad operates **696 servers across 90 countries** as of March 2026. While this is a fraction of NordVPN's 6,800+ or Surfshark's 3,200+ servers, Mullvad's approach prioritizes quality over quantity: - **RAM-only servers**: No data is ever written to disk. If a server is seized or rebooted, all data is permanently erased - **Owned hardware**: Many servers are physically owned by Mullvad rather than rented from third-party hosting providers, reducing supply chain risk - **10 Gbps connections**: The network focuses on high-bandwidth 10 Gbps server connections for optimal performance - **No virtual servers**: Every server is a bare-metal physical machine in the advertised country ### Kill Switch Mullvad's kill switch is **always on and cannot be disabled**. This is an intentional design choice — the company believes there is no legitimate reason to allow unencrypted traffic to leak outside the VPN tunnel. If the VPN connection drops, all internet traffic is immediately blocked. Our testing confirmed this works flawlessly with zero leaks detected across multiple deliberate disconnection tests. DNS leak tests were equally clean: **no DNS leaks, no WebRTC leaks, and no IPv6 leaks** were detected on any server we tested. ### Jurisdiction Mullvad is based in **Gothenburg, Sweden**. Sweden is a member of the EU and part of the 14 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance, which is often cited as a concern by privacy advocates. However, Mullvad's architecture makes jurisdiction largely irrelevant — the company does not collect or store any user data, so there is nothing to share with intelligence agencies regardless of legal requirements. The 2023 police raid proved this in practice. ## Streaming Performance This is Mullvad's biggest weakness, and the company does not pretend otherwise. Mullvad does not market itself as a streaming VPN and does not invest resources into maintaining unblocked access to geo-restricted content. ### Netflix In our testing, Mullvad **failed to unblock Netflix** in any region. Every server we tried was detected and blocked by Netflix's proxy detection system. Independent testing from Top10VPN, Comparitech, and multiple other review sites confirms the same result: Mullvad does not work with Netflix as of March 2026. Some smaller review sites have reported occasional success with specific server locations, but this appears to be inconsistent and unreliable. Mullvad does not actively rotate its IP addresses to bypass streaming blocks, so any access that works today may stop working tomorrow. ### Other Streaming Platforms | Platform | Works? | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | Netflix | No | Blocked across all tested regions | | Disney+ | No | Proxy detected on all servers | | BBC iPlayer | No | Blocked consistently | | Amazon Prime Video | No | Detected and restricted | | Hulu | No | US servers blocked | | HBO Max | Inconsistent | Occasionally works on select US servers | Mullvad does offer a workaround suggestion on its official help page: use **split tunneling** to route streaming traffic outside the VPN tunnel while keeping everything else encrypted. This works but defeats the purpose of using a VPN for geo-unblocking. ### The Bottom Line on Streaming If streaming geo-restricted content is even a secondary use case for you, Mullvad is the wrong choice. NordVPN, Surfshark, or ExpressVPN are far better options for streaming. Mullvad is built for privacy, not entertainment. ## Pricing & Plans Mullvad's pricing is the simplest in the entire VPN industry, and that simplicity is a feature. ### Current Pricing (March 2026) | Plan | Monthly Cost | Billing Period | Total | Renewal | |------|-------------|----------------|-------|---------| | Monthly | **€5.00** | 1 month | €5.00 | €5/month | | 12 Months | **€5.00/mo** | 12 months | €60.00 | €60/year | | 10 Years | **€5.00/mo** | 120 months | €600.00 | €5/month | That is it. No tiers, no upsells, no "Starter vs Plus vs Ultimate" confusion. Every subscriber gets the exact same product at the exact same price. And the price has **never increased since Mullvad launched in 2009** — a remarkable feat of pricing consistency. At current exchange rates, €5 equals approximately **$5.50 USD, £4.34 GBP, or SEK 54**. ### Cryptocurrency Discount Payments made with Bitcoin, Bitcoin Cash, or Monero receive a **10% discount** due to lower processing fees. This brings the effective price down to €4.50 per month — the cheapest way to use Mullvad. ### Payment Methods Mullvad accepts an unusually wide range of payment options: - **Cash**: Send EUR, USD, GBP, SEK, NOK, CHF, CAD, AUD, or NZD in an envelope to their Swedish office - **Cryptocurrency**: Bitcoin, Bitcoin Cash, Monero (with 10% discount) - **Traditional**: Credit card, PayPal, bank wire, Swish, iDEAL, Bancontact, EPS, Przelewy24 The cash and Monero options are unique among VPN providers and allow for completely anonymous payment with zero digital trail. ### How It Compares The €5 flat rate looks expensive compared to introductory offers from competitors — NordVPN starts at €3.39/month and Surfshark at €2.41/month on two-year plans. But here is the crucial difference: **Mullvad's price never changes**.
Pro Tip: Before choosing based on headline price alone, check our best VPN guide — it compares total 3-year costs for every major provider, revealing that Mullvad's flat rate often beats the "cheaper" options once renewal prices kick in.
NordVPN's introductory rate jumps to roughly €8-12/month on renewal. Surfshark's €2.41 becomes €8.25/month after the first term. Over a three-year period, Mullvad's total cost (€180) is actually competitive with NordVPN Plus (€163 first term + ~€144 renewal = €307) and Surfshark One (€81 first term + ~€119 renewal = €200). ### Money-Back Guarantee Mullvad offers a **14-day money-back guarantee** for all payment methods except cash and cryptocurrency (due to anti-money laundering regulations). This is shorter than the 30-day guarantees offered by NordVPN, Surfshark, and ExpressVPN. ## Apps & Usability Mullvad provides native apps for all major platforms: - **Desktop**: Windows, macOS, Linux - **Mobile**: Android, iOS - **No browser extensions** available - **No smart TV or streaming device apps** - **Router support**: Compatible via WireGuard configuration files The app interface is deliberately minimalist. There is no colorful world map, no server speed indicators, and no smart server selection algorithm. You see a list of countries, you pick one, you connect. The design philosophy mirrors the product itself: functional, transparent, no unnecessary complexity. ### Key Features - **Always-on kill switch**: Cannot be disabled, blocks all traffic if VPN drops - **Split tunneling**: Available on Windows, Linux, and Android — route specific apps outside the VPN tunnel - **Multihop (Double VPN)**: Route traffic through two servers for additional privacy layers - **DNS content blocker**: Block ads, trackers, and malware at the DNS level - **SOCKS5 proxy**: Browser-based proxy for lightweight traffic routing - **Custom DNS**: Choose between Mullvad's private DNS, Cloudflare, or Quad9 - **Obfuscation**: Disguise VPN traffic as regular HTTPS traffic to bypass VPN blocking - **Bridge mode**: Route through Shadowsocks proxies for restrictive networks (China, Iran, etc.) - **IPv6 support**: Full native IPv6 support on all platforms ### Device Limit Mullvad allows a maximum of **5 simultaneous connections** per account. This is the industry minimum — NordVPN allows 10, and Surfshark offers unlimited. For individuals, 5 devices is usually sufficient. For households with multiple users, it can become a limitation quickly. ### Linux Support Mullvad deserves special mention for its **Linux support**, which is among the best in the industry. The Linux app is a full-featured native application, not a stripped-down CLI tool. It includes the GUI interface, all features available on Windows and macOS, and deep integration with WireGuard at the kernel level. For Linux users, Mullvad is often considered the top choice. ### Open Source All Mullvad apps are **fully open source** and available on GitHub. This allows independent security researchers to audit the code at any time and verify that the apps behave as claimed. Combined with the regular professional audits, this provides an exceptionally high level of transparency. ## Torrenting and P2P Mullvad is an excellent choice for torrenting. All servers support P2P traffic with no restrictions, bandwidth caps, or throttling. The always-on kill switch ensures your real IP address is never exposed, even if the VPN connection drops momentarily. The combination of anonymous account creation, no-logs policy (verified by multiple audits), and cash/crypto payment options makes Mullvad one of the most private VPNs available for torrenting. There is genuinely no way for Mullvad — or anyone else — to link torrenting activity back to your real identity if you pay with cash or Monero. Port forwarding is **not supported** as of 2026. Mullvad removed this feature in July 2023, citing abuse concerns. This may impact download speeds for some torrent clients that rely on port forwarding for optimal performance. ## Who Should Use Mullvad? Mullvad is the right VPN for: - **Journalists and whistleblowers**: The anonymous account system and cash payment option provide genuine protection for sources and sensitive communications - **Privacy researchers and security professionals**: Mullvad's open-source code, audit history, and transparent architecture make it the go-to recommendation in the security community - **Linux users**: Best-in-class Linux support with a full native GUI application - **Users in restrictive countries**: Bridge mode and obfuscation features help bypass VPN blocking in China, Iran, and similar environments - **Torrenters who prioritize anonymity**: No-logs, no identity, no payment trail — maximum privacy for P2P activity - **Anyone tired of VPN pricing games**: One price, forever, no surprises ## Who Should NOT Use Mullvad? - **Streamers**: Mullvad cannot reliably unblock Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, or any major streaming platform. Use NordVPN or Surfshark instead - **Large households**: The 5-device limit is restrictive for families. Surfshark's unlimited connections are a better fit - **Users who want maximum speed**: Mullvad's mid-tier speeds lag behind NordVPN and ExpressVPN, especially on long-distance connections - **Beginners who want a simple experience**: The minimalist interface and lack of automatic server selection may frustrate users accustomed to more polished VPN apps - **Users who need customer support**: Mullvad offers email support only — no live chat, no phone support, no 24/7 availability - **Anyone who needs browser extensions**: Mullvad does not offer Chrome, Firefox, or Edge extensions ## FAQ **Is Mullvad VPN safe to use in 2026?** Yes. Mullvad is one of the safest VPNs available. It has undergone approximately eight independent security audits from firms including Cure53, Assured AB, X41 D-Sec GmbH, and NCC Group. All audits found no critical or high-severity vulnerabilities. The apps are fully open source, the servers are RAM-only, and many are physically owned by Mullvad. A 2023 police raid in Sweden confirmed that no user data existed on Mullvad's systems. **Does Mullvad work with Netflix?** No. As of March 2026, Mullvad does not reliably unblock Netflix in any region. Netflix's proxy detection system blocks Mullvad's servers consistently. If streaming is important to you, consider NordVPN or Surfshark, which both unblock 30+ Netflix libraries. **Why is Mullvad more expensive than NordVPN or Surfshark?** Mullvad's flat €5 per month looks expensive compared to introductory rates of €2-3 per month from competitors. However, those introductory prices double or triple upon renewal. Over three years, Mullvad's total cost (€180) is actually competitive with or cheaper than competitors when renewal pricing is factored in. Mullvad has never raised its price since launching in 2009. **Does Mullvad keep any logs?** No. Mullvad's no-logs policy has been independently verified by multiple audit firms. The company does not store connection timestamps, IP addresses, bandwidth usage, or any browsing activity. Because Mullvad does not collect email addresses or personal information during signup, it has no way to link VPN usage to individual users even if legally compelled. **How many devices can I use with Mullvad?** Mullvad allows up to 5 simultaneous device connections per account. You can install the app on unlimited devices, but only 5 can be connected at the same time. This is lower than NordVPN (10 devices) and Surfshark (unlimited devices). **Can I use Mullvad in China?** Yes, with caveats. Mullvad offers bridge mode using Shadowsocks proxies and traffic obfuscation features designed to bypass VPN blocking in restrictive countries like China, Iran, and Russia. However, the Great Firewall is constantly evolving, and no VPN can guarantee 100% reliability in China. Mullvad's obfuscation features are regularly updated to maintain access. ## How Mullvad Compares Wondering how Mullvad stacks up against the competition? Check out our head-to-head comparisons:
  • Mullvad vs Proton VPN — anonymous account numbers vs Proton's Swiss jurisdiction and free tier
  • Mullvad vs NordVPN — radical privacy with flat-rate pricing vs speed and streaming excellence
  • Mullvad vs IVPN — two audited no-email VPNs compared on price, features, and anonymity
## Sources ## Related Reviews Explore more VPN reviews from our testing lab:
  • Proton VPN Review — the closest privacy-focused competitor — Swiss jurisdiction, open-source, but also great for streaming
  • NordVPN Review — if you want the best streaming VPN without sacrificing security, NordVPN is the obvious alternative
  • Surfshark VPN Review — unlimited devices and solid privacy audits at $1.99/mo — a good middle ground if Mullvad's streaming gap is a dealbreaker
